member.name: T:Testify.AssertExpectation`1
member.summary: Describes how one quoted value should be validated, rendered, and explained when it fails.

remarks.para: An AssertExpectation is reusable. Build it once, then pass it to Assert.check, Assert.should, or the Assert operator DSL.
In the DSL, expectations are usually applied with |>?, composed with <|> and <&>, or used in readable multi-expectation lists with ||? and &&?.

remarks.para: Testify fills this record when an Assert operation does not satisfy its expectation. The values are already formatted for diagnostics and report rendering.

member.name: M:Testify.CheckFailure`3.TryGetReplayConfig
member.summary: Attempts to reconstruct an FsCheck replay configuration for the failing case.
member.returns: Some replay configuration when the stored replay token can be parsed; otherwise None.
member.remarks: Use this to rerun a property-style check with the same generated data that produced the recorded failure.

member.name: T:Testify.CheckFailure`3
member.summary: Structured information describing a failing property-style test case.
member.remarks: A CheckFailure captures the shrunk counterexample, rendered values, and optional replay information needed to understand or reproduce a failing property-style check.

member.name: M:Testify.AssertExpectation.satisfyObserved``1(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpResult{``0,System.Exception},System.Boolean})
member.summary: Builds an expectation from a predicate over the fully observed result.

The predicate that receives the full Observed value, including exceptions.
member.returns: A reusable expectation based on the supplied observed-result predicate.

member.summary: Builds an expectation from a predicate over successful values.
member.param: A short human-readable description of the expected behavior.
The predicate that must return true for successful observed values.
member.returns: A reusable expectation based on the supplied predicate.
member.remarks: Use satisfy when you only care about the resulting value. If you need to inspect thrown exceptions as well, use satisfyObserved.

member.name: T:Testify.Assert.Collector
member.summary: Opaque collector that stores multiple assertion results for later aggregation.
member.remarks: Collectors are useful when you want several independent assertions to run and report together instead of stopping at the first failure.

member.name: M:Testify.Assert.checkAsync``1(Testify.AssertExpectation{``0},Microsoft.FSharp.Quotations.FSharpExpr)
member.summary: Evaluates an asynchronous assertion and returns the result inside a task.
member.param: The reusable expectation to verify.
The quoted asynchronous expression under test, typically producing Async<'T>, Task<'T>, or another awaitable value supported by Testify.
member.returns: A task that completes with Passed when the asynchronous computation satisfies the expectation, or Failed when it does not.
member.remarks: The quoted asynchronous computation is awaited by this call.

member.name: M:Testify.Assert.checkNamed``1(System.String,Testify.AssertExpectation{``0},Microsoft.FSharp.Quotations.FSharpExpr{``0})
member.summary: Evaluates an assertion like check, but uses the supplied test label in failure output.
member.param: The human-readable label to show instead of the rendered quotation.
The reusable expectation to verify.
The quoted expression under test.
member.returns: Passed when the quoted expression satisfies the expectation; otherwise a structured Failed result that uses in its report output.
member.remarks: The quotation is still evaluated by this call even when a custom label is supplied.

member.name: M:Testify.Assert.check``1(Testify.AssertExpectation{``0},Microsoft.FSharp.Quotations.FSharpExpr{``0})
member.summary: Evaluates an assertion expectation against a quoted expression.
member.param: The reusable expectation to verify.
The quoted expression under test.
member.returns: Passed when the quoted expression satisfies the expectation; otherwise a structured Failed result describing the mismatch.

remarks.para: The quotation is evaluated by this call. Use check when you want to inspect the result yourself instead of failing immediately.

member.name: M:Testify.Assert.Collect.assertAll(Testify.Assert.Collector)
member.summary: Raises once when any collected assertion failed.

member.summary: Runs one assertion, then returns the original quotation for further chaining.
member.param: The quoted expression under test.
The expectation to apply.
member.returns: The original quoted expression.
member.exception: Raised when does not satisfy .

remarks.para: This is the chain-friendly Assert operator. It supports readable left-to-right pipelines while preserving fail-fast behavior.
The returned quotation is not cached. If it is checked again later in the chain, it will be evaluated again.

member.name: T:Testify.AssertOperators
member.summary: Concise operator syntax for example-based assertions.

remarks.para: The Assert DSL is expression-left: the quoted value stays on the left and the asserted logic reads from left to right.

Recommended usage: |>? for one expectation, <|> and <&> to compose a few reusable expectations, ||? when any expectation from a sequence may pass, and &&? when every expectation from a sequence must pass.
Assert operators are fail-fast. They raise on the first failing assertion instead of collecting multiple failures.

member.param: The collector whose stored results should be validated.

member.remarks: This lets you accumulate independent property-check failures and report them together after all desired checks have run.

member.name: T:Testify.Check.Collect
member.summary: Helpers for collecting multiple independent property-check results.
member.remarks: Collector helpers use the non-throwing check APIs internally. Results are stored in insertion order and no exception is raised until assertAll is called.

member.summary: Advanced convenience operator for equality/reference checks with optional configuration, arbitrary, and expectation.
member.param: The quoted implementation under test.
A tuple containing an optional FsCheck configuration, an optional arbitrary, an optional expectation, and the reference implementation.
member.exception: Raised when the resolved check fails.

remarks.para: This operator is a power-user shortcut rather than the primary recommended API.

When the configuration is omitted, Testify uses CheckConfig.defaultConfig. When the arbitrary is omitted, Testify resolves the default arbitrary for the input type. When the expectation is omitted, Testify uses CheckExpectation.equalToReference.

member.summary: Runs a custom property-style check, then returns the original quotation for further chaining.
member.param: The quoted implementation under test.
A tuple containing the custom expectation and the reference implementation.
member.returns: The original quoted implementation.
member.exception: Raised when the check fails.
member.remarks: The returned quotation is not cached. If it is checked again later in the chain, it will be evaluated again.

member.name: T:Testify.CheckOperators
member.summary: Concise operator syntax for property-style checks.
member.remarks: These operators keep the quoted implementation on the left while delegating to the pipe-friendly Check function API underneath. Bool-returning property tests intentionally use named helpers such as Check.shouldBeTrue and Check.shouldBeFalse instead of a dedicated operator.
